stories.headline: Trump imposes new double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
stories.summary: With existing 10 percent levies set to expire, Trump issues new tariffs on 60 countries under forced labour provision.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Trump imposes new tariffs as EU agrees fresh sanctions on Russia
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump is imposing a new wave of tariffs on trading partners, including the EU, with levies ranging between 10 and 12.5%. Bernd Lange, the EU Parliament top lawmaker on trade, reacts to this measure. Also in Brussels: The EU has agreed a new round of sanctions against Russia.

stories.headline: Once a textile powerhouse, Syria is betting on an industrial comeback
stories.summary: Syria once produced garments that reached Arab and European markets, but the civil war dismantled the industry over the years. With sanctions lifted and investors beginning to return, Aleppo's first major trade fair in years is testing whether that world can be rebuilt.

stories.headline: US imposes new tariffs on dozens of countries over 'forced labour' claims
stories.summary: The newly-announced tariffs, ranging from 10 to 12.5%, will replace expiring global tariffs on Friday announced by Trump earlier this year.

stories.headline: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

stories.headline: US hits 60 countries with new duties as Trump rebuilds tariff wall
stories.summary: Washington pins measures on forced labour probe after Supreme Court struck down blanket levies

stories.headline: UK Pub Stocks Fall as Heat Wave Proves Too Hot For Football Fans
stories.summary: UK pub stocks slumped this week as a sales boost from the World Cup failed to offset the drag from a brutal heat wave and higher costs.

stories.headline: Japan’s Katayama Says to Take Bold FX Steps Decisively as Needed
stories.summary: Japanese authorities will take bold steps to counter moves in the currency market decisively as needed, and a US Treasury report issued overnight underscores a shared view that excessive currency moves are undesirable, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

stories.headline: PBOC Adds Most Liquidity to Economy in Five Months to Aid Growth
stories.summary: China’s central bank made its biggest addition of liquidity to the economy in five months via its medium-term lending facility to support growth ahead of its late July Politburo meeting and to accommodate government bond issuance.

stories.headline: Korean Stocks Sink as Chipmakers Drop on Middle East, China IPO
stories.summary: South Korean stocks fell as funds cut exposure to chipmakers after overnight losses in US peers, while factoring in a Chinese rival’s imminent share sale and rising Middle East tensions.

stories.headline: India’s Markets Shrug Off Student Protests for Now

stories.headline: Los Angeles Homeless Population Grows Again, After 2 Years of Decline

stories.headline: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries

stories.headline: Democratic Voters Keep Defying the Party Establishment

stories.headline: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape

stories.headline: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
